title: Reliability Engineering for Systems That Don't Fail Loudly
excerpt: WP-Cron failures rarely announce themselves. They quietly stop scheduled imports, stale listings accumulate, and support tickets arrive days later. Reliability often means designing around silent failure.
body: WordPress's built-in cron system works until it doesn't.
And when it stops, it usually stops quietly.
Many integrations depend on recurring schedules.
If the event disappears, nothing breaks loudly.
The symptom may not appear until days later.
The support request arrives after the failure has already occurred.
